purpose: Remedy arm: soft horizon CBF with per-stage slack input and L1 exact penalty (mandated remedy profile 1). Slack stays at zero whenever the hard problem is feasible; when infeasible, the solver returns a least-violation action instead of failing closed.
notes: Runs on the velocity-tube base (the only arm that improved the primary endpoint) so the slack targets that arm's 11.7% solver-failure steps; the static-base variant would inherit the dead-time collisions the slack cannot fix.
Penalty weight 1000 per unit slack (h is in m^2, typical magnitudes <= 0.1): far above observed constraint multipliers, so the penalty is exact and slack activates only under genuine infeasibility.

Slack bounded to [0, 10]; the collision barrier itself is untouched (isotropic Euclidean, radii unchanged) - softness is strictly a feasibility valve, per the never-weaken-the-barrier rule the barrier value h and all true-clearance metrics are computed exactly as before.
Fail-closed path remains for any residual solver failure.
